question: How do I connect a washer and dryer?

result.summary: The relevant manual sections cover safety, washer water and drain hookups, leveling, dryer power or gas connection, dryer venting, final startup checks, and professional help conditions.
findings: Safety setup requires turning off power, water, and gas where applicable.
Washer setup requires matching hot and cold hoses, checking leaks, securing the drain hose, and leveling the unit.
Dryer setup requires the correct electric or gas connection plus a rigid or semi-rigid metal vent.

result.summary: The document supports a sequence of safety shutdown, washer hose and drain setup, leveling, dryer utility connection, vent connection, and final startup checks.
findings: Connect washer water hoses without overtightening and turn water on slowly to check for leaks.
Secure the drain hose without sealing the standpipe airtight.
Run startup checks after both appliances are in position.

result.summary: The document repeatedly says to stop and contact a qualified professional for uncertain electrical, gas, plumbing, venting, leak, smell, sparking, or abnormal vibration issues.
findings: Do not proceed with damaged cords, crushed hoses, kinked vents, or leaking gas connectors.
Use approved leak-detection solution for gas dryer checks.
Call a professional for outlet changes, gas line work, shutoff valve issues, supply line leaks, or new vent routes.

answer: To connect a washer and dryer, first turn off power, water, and gas if applicable. Connect the washer hot and cold hoses to the matching inlets, turn water on slowly, and check for leaks. Secure the washer drain hose in the standpipe or laundry sink without sealing it airtight. Level the washer so it is stable. For the dryer, connect the correct electrical cord or approved gas connector according to the appliance manual and local code. Attach a rigid or semi-rigid metal dryer vent, keep it short and straight, and confirm the outside vent opens. Run a short washer cycle and a low-heat or air-only dryer cycle to check for leaks, drainage, airflow, noise, or smells.
steps: Turn off electrical power, water supply, and gas supply if installing a gas dryer.
Connect hot and cold washer hoses to the matching inlets and check for leaks.
Secure the washer drain hose without creating an airtight seal.
Level the washer and tighten lock nuts if included.
Connect the dryer power cord or approved gas connector according to the appliance manual and local code.
Attach a rigid or semi-rigid metal dryer vent and confirm airflow outside.
Run startup checks for leaks, drainage, airflow, unusual noise, or smells.
safety_notes: Do not use damaged power cords, crushed hoses, kinked vents, or leaking gas connectors.
If plug, outlet, wiring, gas, plumbing, or venting work is uncertain, contact a qualified professional.
Stop using the appliance if there is burning smell, gas smell, water leak, sparking, repeated breaker trips, or abnormal vibration.
agent_consensus: All three agents agree the manual supports a safety-first installation sequence with washer connections, dryer utility and vent setup, final checks, and clear professional escalation conditions.
